title: Chamelum frigidum
intro: Chamelum frigidum, also known as frostweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and fever, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Chamelum frigidum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Chamelum frigidum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic properties.
